p2pErpSignals.controlSummary: Gap- While a compensating controls exist within the procure-to-pay process, there is currently no formal, documented review of additions, deletions, or modifications to vendor master data within Sage. Changes to the vendor master file are not consistently reviewed or approved by the Senior GL accountant and evidence of such review is retained via email. Because these controls are manual and not operating as designed within the ERP, they do not sufficiently mitigate the risk that unauthorized, inaccurate, or inappropriate vendor information may be created or modified. Below is the proposed control for remediation: All changes to the vendor data in Sage (i.e. additions/delete/modifications) are reviewed and approved by the Senior GL Accountant. Evidence of review is via email. Also refer to FR-PTP-03 for data validation checks by Trustpair. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation, as no functionality in Sage 100 to produce reporting.
p2pErpSignals.risk: Unauthorized or fictitious changes are made to vendor master data, leading to fraudulent or unauthorized disbursements.

p2pErpSignals.controlSummary: All new purchase orders are initiated in DocuShare by the requestor and manually routed for approval based on a pre-defined drop down approval list configured within the system. Gap- Although purchase orders are initiated in DocuShare and routed for approval based on a predefined drop-down approval list, the system does not prevent users from approving their own purchase orders, even when they are included in the DOA approval hierarchy. While instances of self-approval can be identified after the fact, the lack of a system-enforced segregation of duties results in an unmitigated risk that POs may be self-approved without appropriate oversight. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation.
p2pErpSignals.risk: Purchase Orders are not authorized, resulting in insufficient oversight from those delegated with authorization responsibilities. There are financial obligations the Company is unaware of, resulting in a misstatement in expenses.

p2pErpSignals.controlSummary: Gap- The Company does not currently perform a periodic review of open orders to identify items that have remained outstanding beyond a defined threshold. As a result, there is no process in place to ensure that long-outstanding orders are timely investigated and resolved. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Monthly, the AP Accountant, runs an exception report for all open orders from DocuShare. All open orders outstanding for over 2 months are investigated and resolved. The exception workbook is reviewed and approved by the Senior GL accountant/Accounting Director. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation. No functionality in DocuShare to produce reporting.
p2pErpSignals.risk: Vendor invoices are recorded untimely, inaccurately, or incompletely.

p2pErpSignals.controlSummary: a) Purchase Order-based invoice: A manual 2-way match (PO/Invoice) is performed by the Accounts Payable. Discrepancies [based on a percentage and amount, as defined by Management are investigated and resolved with the operations team prior to manual entry into the DocuShare system. Gap- There is currently no automated two-way match in DocuShare and the manual 2-way match may result in errors; therefore, there remains an unmitigated risk that discrepancies may go undetected. There is an increased risk that inaccurate, duplicate, or unauthorized invoices may be processed or paid, resulting in potential financial misstatement, improper payment, or fraud. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation. b) Non-Purchase Order-based Invoice are approved in accordance with an approved delegation of authority within DocuShare.
p2pErpSignals.risk: Unauthorized invoices are processed, resulting in those delegated with authorization responsibilities having insufficient oversight. Expenditures are recorded/coded incompletely, inaccurately, or to the incorrect GL account, resulting in misstatements to the expense accounts. Fictitious/unauthorized or erroneous invoices are approved for payments.
p2pErpSignals.reviewer: Various (Approvers in line with an approved delegation of authority within DocuShare )

p2pErpSignals.controlSummary: Gap- The Company does not currently perform a formal Search for Unrecorded Liabilities. Key steps, such as evaluating subsequent disbursements against the defined thresholds and documenting conclusions, are not reliably formalized. In this way, there is an unmitigated risk that unrecorded liabilities may not be identified or accrued in the proper period. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the AP Accountant prepares the Search for Unrecorded Liabilities Analysis to determine if subsequent disbursements / invoices greater than [based on a percentage and amount, as defined by Management] were properly included or excluded from the quarterly accruals. The final Search for Unrecorded Liabilities Analysis is reviewed and approved by the Senior GL Accountant and Director of FP&A. Evidence of review is via comments throughout the file, tickmarks, sign-off and/or email communication. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation.
p2pErpSignals.risk: The Company does not know it has an obligation and therefore does not record a liability. Liabilities are not recognized in the correct period, resulting in a misstatement.
p2pErpSignals.reviewer: Senior GL Accountant and Director of FP&A

p2pErpSignals.controlSummary: Gap- The quarterly Sage user access review is not being performed or documented as designed. As a result, key procedures, including validating user access appropriateness, confirming timely removal of terminated employees, and reviewing administrator access, are not executed or evidenced. These activities are manual and inconsistent, and therefore there remains an unmitigated risk unauthorized, inappropriate, or conflicting access within Sage may go undetected. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the GL Accountant performs the following administrator user access review procedures for Sage: 1. Reviews Sage User Access Reports to ensure user access is commensurate with user responsibilities and there is no conflicting access. 2. Compares the User Access Reports to listing of terminated users from HR to ensure that all terminated employees are listed as inactive and do not retain access to in scope systems past their termination date. 3. Reviews administrator access listings for appropriateness, as applicable. 4. If exceptions are identified, lookback procedures to evaluate the root cause and impact of the exception are performed and documented to ensure access is modified/removed following the corresponding formal process. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, comments, and email on the review. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation.
p2pErpSignals.risk: Application end-users and other high-risk/powerful accounts (e.g., super-user, system administrators, database administrators etc.) bypass systems-enforced authorization and segregation of duties controls, resulting in inappropriate transactions, unauthorized activities, or improper direct changes being made to underlying transaction records or master data

reportingGapSignals.controlSummary: Gap- While the Company formally prepares a budget to actuals analysis, the Company has not defined the level of precision used to identify inconsistencies. As such there remains an unmitigated risk that errors or inconsistencies exist in the financials and are not identified. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Head of FP&A prepares the Budget vs Actuals (BvA) Analysis to compare actual progress against the Board-approved budget to identify unusual or unexpected activity above [$XX and XX%- Management to confirm threshold]. The BvA Analysis is reviewed/approved by the Head of the Departments and CFO. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the workbooks.
reportingGapSignals.risk: Budget-to-Actual review is not performed periodically at a level of precision to identify inconsistencies or irregularities in the financial statements. There is insufficient oversight from the Board of Directors in monitoring the Company's progress against approved metrics.
reportingGapSignals.remediationPlan: Gap- create a threshold for investigation

reportingGapSignals.controlSummary: Gap- Sage 100 has an identified material weakness, and the system does not include a built-in approval workflow to ensure proper segregation of duties between preparers and reviewers. Users with posting access can prepare, post, and review journal entries without independent approval. In addition, Sage 100 does not allow supporting documentation to be attached directly to journal entries. As a result, there is an increased risk that unauthorized, unsupported, or erroneous journal entries could be recorded in the general ledger. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, all manual journal entries posted within Sage 100 are reviewed by the Accounting Director. The review is performed using an exported journal entry report by user ID and includes identification of self-approved entries, entries posted by unauthorized users, high-dollar or unusual transactions, and judgmentally selected entries for detailed support review. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the workbook. Abivax to consider manual control for 2026 - TBD by Management Note For Abivax: Confirm this is a Journal Entry Detail/Transactional based report - this should be reconciled (if not monthly, quarterly) against the GL TB for completeness purposes, considering that SOD/create and post/ERP MW. Combining the review for authorization/supporting detail, review of create/post and inappropriate JEs entry, and the third party requirement to post entries to a closed period, along with BS reconciliations will support accuracy, authorization, and any anti-fraud.
reportingGapSignals.risk: Fictitious and/or fraudulent journal entries are recorded. Journal entries posted into the general ledger/sub-ledger are not supported and are recorded without proper approval. Inaccurate journal entries are recorded that result in a material misstatement.
reportingGapSignals.remediationPlan: Material weakness within Sage 100 that does not include a built-in approval workflow to ensure proper segregation of duties between preparers and reviewers.

reportingGapSignals.controlSummary: Access to open a prior period in the general ledger is restricted to the Sage 100 third-party administrator. The Senior GL Accountant or GL Accountant obtains email approval from the Accounting Director to open the period and post the entries. Following approval, the third-party administrator opens the period, allowing the Senior GL Accountant or GL Accountant to post the necessary journal entries. All prior period adjustments are reviewed by the Accounting Director. The Senior GL Accountant or GL Accountant closes the period and provides notification to the Accounting Director via email.
reportingGapSignals.risk: Fictitious and/or fraudulent journal entries are posted to a closed period. Journal entries posted to closed periods result in transactions that are recorded in the inappropriate period.

reportingGapSignals.controlSummary: Gap- The Company does not document materiality thresholds to guide the level of investigation and review performed for key financial analyses (e.g., Budget/Forecast to Actual Analysis, Balance Sheet Account Reconciliations, Flux Analysis, and Significant Transactions). As a result, there is no consistent or risk-based framework to determine which variances or transactions require further investigation and management review. As a result, there remains an unmitigated risk that material misstatements, unusual trends, or significant variances may not be identified, analyzed, or escalated on a timely basis. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Annually, the Head of FP&A establishes materiality thresholds for specific financial analyses performed throughout the year (Budget/Forecast to Actual Analysis, Balance Sheet Account Reconciliations, Flux Analysis, Significant Transactions) by defining a percentage and dollar value material threshold for variances noted. The results of the materiality thresholds are documented in a materiality memo, which is reviewed and approved by the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via comments throughout the memo and signoff or email signoff.
reportingGapSignals.risk: Account balances are not reviewed at sufficient level of precision to identify errors and/or material misstatements. Significant variances are not investigated/reviewed and materially significant inaccuracies are included in the financial statements.

reportingGapSignals.controlSummary: Gap - The Company reviews the work of the third-party however, there is no formal evidence retained that supports the adequacy of management reviews procedures and oversight of third parties. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that the third-party has errors in the file or does not have institutional knowledge to include all relevant transactions. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the third-party provider performs the manual consolidation of the LLC (US) and SA (FR) entities. The consolidation includes adjustments for intercompany eliminations, and alignment IFRS adjustments. The workbook is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director and the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.
reportingGapSignals.risk: Consolidated Financial Statement are not accurate nor complete.
reportingGapSignals.remediationPlan: Gap - Formal evidence of review is not retained.

reportingGapSignals.controlSummary: Quarterly, the balance sheet and income statement flux analyses are prepared by the Head of FP&A, to identify unexpected and/or unusual activity. Significant variances (as defined by Management) are investigated, resolved, and documented within the analyses by the Head of FP&A. The flux analyses are reviewed and approved by the SVP Finance. GAP - No evidence of review of the flux analysis is retained. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes and sign-off within the consolidation workbooks.
reportingGapSignals.risk: Errors or anomalies in financial statements are not identified and investigated/resolved, resulting in misstatements. Flux analyses are not reviewed at a level of precision to identify material errors and/or misstatements.
reportingGapSignals.remediationPlan: Gap- evidence of review to be retained

reportingGapSignals.controlSummary: Quarterly, prior to the issuance of the 6-K/20-F, the Disclosure Committee independently reviews the financial statements and all footnote disclosures. Evidence of review is maintained within Workiva and via a signed disclosure checklist.
reportingGapSignals.risk: Those charged with governance do not have sufficient oversight over the organizational activities, resulting in oversight gaps.

reportingGapSignals.controlSummary: Gap- While the Company performs a tie-out of the Form 20-F and Form 6-K to ensure the accuracy of financial information and related disclosures, the process is not formally documented, and evidence of review is not consistently retained. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that tie-out procedures and related management reviews may not be performed consistently across reporting periods, resulting in potential errors, omissions, or misstatements in the financial statements and footnote disclosures. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Finance Manager performs a tie out of the 20-F/6-K including balance sheet, income statement, statement of stockholders’ equity, statement of cash flows, and footnote disclosures, as evidenced by tickmarks and review notes within the work file. The tie-out is reviewed and approved by the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via signoff on the financial statements and tickmarks/notations throughout the report.
reportingGapSignals.risk: The financial statements are not presented in accordance with the prescribed guidance. Balances are not appropriately or accurately presented in the statement of cash flows. The presented events, transactions, management assertions, and other matters may not have occurred or pertain to the Company.
reportingGapSignals.remediationPlan: Gap - Formal evidence of review is not retained.

records.controlTitle: Monthly CMO Meeting w/Supply Chain Team (On Quarter Ending Months)
records.controlSummary: Gap-While the CMC Team holds monthly meetings with department managers to review study progress, CRO data, contract milestones, and the completeness of the CRO vendor/contract listing, meeting minutes are not currently documented. As a result, there is no formal record of discussions, decisions, follow-up items, or conclusions reached during these meetings. This results in an unmitigated risk that key issues, updates, or required actions may not be contemplated as part of the accrual. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Formally, on quarter ending months, the CMC Team and representatives from contract manufacturers meet to discuss the progress of the production schedule and activity to date. Supply Chain Team validates any work noted to be completed by the contract manufacturers against the batch records, certificate of analysis, etc. Evidence to support the meeting include meeting minutes and/or email communication that is documented and shared with the Director of FP&A and saved within the accruals workbook.

Quarterly, the Director of FP&A accountant prepares and the Accounting Director reviews and approves the CMC Accruals Workbook. Evidence of review is via comments throughout the workbook by the Accounting Director. Also refer to FSCP-C12 'Monthly, the Head of FP&A prepares the G&A accruals with the support of Head of Department and operational staff. The Senior Manager of Accounting performs a detailed review of the workbook to verify the accuracy of the data, consistency with supporting documentation, and ensuring that numbers are correct. The Accounting Director performs a secondary review for approval. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.'

records.controlTitle: Clinical Trial and CRO Budget Approval
records.controlSummary: Annually, the Company prepares a clinical trial and CRO budget for the upcoming fiscal year, which is presented to the Board of Directors for approval. Evidence of approval is via meeting minutes or written consent.

records.controlTitle: Clinical Trial and CRO Agreements are reviewed
records.controlSummary: All new or modified clinical trial and CRO agreements are reviewed and approved in accordance with the Delegation of Authority policy. Evidence of approval is via sign-off on the executed contract.

Quarterly, the Director of FP&A reviews the clinical trial and CRO budget versus actuals to track all clinical studies progress and spend against the contractual budget for directional accuracy. The activity of the CROs is validated as part of CTA-04

records.controlTitle: Validation of Third Party Activity Reporting
records.controlSummary: Monthly, clinical trial activity reports are obtained for significant third party Clinical Research Organizations (CROs) (as determined by management), that is reviewed by the Director of FP&A and the Director of Clinical Operations to validate completeness and accuracy of the report data, and verify the clinical activities reported by the CRO are in line with their internal data tracking sheets, the agreed upon budget and Clinical Trial Agreement. Evidence of review is via email and also is performed in conjunction with the quarterly Clinical Accruals Workbook review via comments and tickmarks within the workbook.

records.controlTitle: Monthly CRO Meeting (formally documented on Quarter Ending Months)
records.controlSummary: Gap-While the Director of FP&A and Head of Clinical Trials hold monthly meetings with department managers to review study progress, CRO data, contract milestones, and the completeness of the CRO vendor/contract listing, meeting minutes are not currently documented. As a result, there is no formal record of discussions, decisions, follow-up items, or conclusions reached during these meetings. This results in an unmitigated risk that key issues, updates, or required actions may not be contemplated as part of the accrual. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Formally, on quarter ending months, the Director meet with the Heads of clinical trials, Clinical Project managers and the Translational Research Director to discuss the progress and status of studies, the CRO data reports, any contract milestones met, as well as the completeness of the CRO vendors / contracts listing. Evidence of review and approval is via meeting minutes, notes, and sign-off on the close checklist sign-off.

Quarterly, the Director of FP&A and the Clinical Operations Team prepare the Clinical Accruals Workbook, that is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director. Evidence of review is via comments throughout the workbook, preparation of the journal entries by the Head of FP&A and signoff by the Accounting Director on the Clinical Accruals Review. Also refer to FSCP-C12 'Monthly, the Head of FP&A prepares the G&A accruals with the support of Head of Department and operational staff. The Senior Manager of Accounting performs a detailed review of the workbook to verify the accuracy of the data, consistency with supporting documentation, and ensuring that numbers are correct. The Accounting Director performs a secondary review for approval. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.'

Gap- The Company does not have a formalized control in place to ensure that all significant or non-routine equity transactions are appropriately reviewed and approved by the Board of Directors prior to execution. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that material transactions could be undertaken without proper oversight or authorization, potentially resulting in misstatements in the financial statements. Below is the proposed control for remediation: As needed, all significant, non-routine equity transactions [as defined by management] are approved by the Board of Directors. Evidence of approval is via Board of Directors meeting minutes and/or written consent.

Gap- The Company does not have a formalized process in place to ensure that significant or non-standard equity transactions are subject to appropriate technical accounting analysis and management review prior to recording. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that complex or unusual transactions may not be accounted for in accordance with IFRS, potentially resulting in misstatements in the financial statements or incomplete disclosures. Below is the proposed control for remediation: As needed, for significant, non-routine equity transactions (as defined by Management), a third-party specialist, the Accounting Director and/or SVP Finance prepares a technical assessment and memo that details the transaction and any potential accounting implications. The technical assessment and memo are reviewed by the CFO prior to the recording of the transaction, as evidenced via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off on the analysis.
records.preparer: SVP Finance - TBD by Management Accounting Director - TBD by Management Third party specialist

records.controlSummary: As needed, all new equity plans or amendments to existing equity plans are approved by the Board of Directors. Evidence of approval is via Board of Directors meeting minutes and/or written consent.

All employee equity awards or changes are reviewed and approved by the Remuneration Committee. Evidence of Remuneration Committee approval is retained through meeting minutes and/or written consents. Following this review, the final approval of the equity awards is obtained from the Board of Directors before the grants are issued, which is evidenced through Board meeting minutes or written sign-off.

Gap-The Company does not perform a review of new or modified equity grant data entered into Certent. As a result, there is no verification that the award information (e.g., grant date, number of shares, vesting terms, and exercise price) entered in the system accurately reflects the approved equity grants authorized by the Remuneration Committee and Board of Directors. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Monthly, new/modifications to the equity grant in Certent is reconciled by the Finance Manager. As part of the reconciliation, Certent is compared to the monthly Cap Table. The reconciliation is reviewed and approved by the SVP of Finance. Evidence of review is via email correspondence.

New and/or modified equity award grant data is provided to third party Uptevia by HR France and is reviewed and approved by the CPO to validate completeness and accuracy of the award grant data in the system. Evidence of review and approval is via email correspondence.

Gap-The Company does not have a process in place to monitor or restrict employees from exercising vested stock options during established blackout periods. While Uptevia administers the equity plan information, no detective or preventative control exists to confirm that option exercises are blocked or reviewed for compliance with blackout restrictions. As such, there is an unmitigated risk of non-compliance with insider trading policies and securities regulations. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Accounting Director obtains a report from Uptevia detailing all stock option exercises during the period. The report is reviewed to verify that no exercises occurred during established blackout periods, in accordance with the Company’s insider trading policy, by the CFO. Any exceptions identified are investigated and escalated to management for resolution. Evidence of review is maintained via annotated reports and/or email correspondence. This control is still open and under discussion by Abivax Management.
Uptevia restricts all employees from exercising stock options that are not vested.

Certent: Gap- The Finance Manager only verifies employee terminations during the offboarding checklist review, however there is no documented review or evidence that the process was executed. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that terminated employees may remain active in the equity systems, leading to unauthorized vesting, inaccurate stock-based compensation calculations, or misstated financial reporting. Below is the proposed control for remediation: As required, the Finance Manager updates Certent for employee terminations after receiving the offboarding checklist (part of control TBC), as per termination dates per the notification to verify the option cancellation / forfeiture was processed completely and accurately and during the requisite period. The SVP Finance reviews the cancelled / forfeited options in Certent as a part of the Equity Plans Movement reconciliation review, as evidenced via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off on the file.

Uptevia: As required, the Chief People Officer (CPO) updates Uptevia for employee terminations to verify the option cancellation / forfeiture was processed completely and accurately and during the requisite period. The SVP Finance reviews the cancelled / forfeited options in Uptevia as a part of the Equity Plans Movement reconciliation review, as evidenced via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off on the file.
Stock Compensation Expense calculations are automatically performed by Certent. Also refer to control FR-EQ-13.

Gap - Currently, for new or modified equity awards, the Company engages a Third-Party Consultant to prepare the Black Scholes Valuation assumptions calculations (e.g., volatility, expected term). However, there is insufficient evidence of review procedures performed and/or retained over the Black Scholes assumptions that are input into Certent to determine the fair value of options. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that the fair value of options are inaccurate resulting in a misstatement in stock-based compensation expense and related GL accounts. Below is the proposed control for recommendation: Quarterly, the Finance Manager prepares the Black Scholes Valuation Model, which is reviewed/approved by the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via email correspondence, tickmarks and/or commentary throughout the file.

Monthly, the Accounting Director reconciles all equity plan movements (e.g., grants, exercises, cancellations, and forfeitures) to the Company’s capitalization table to ensure completeness and accuracy of recorded equity transactions, which is prepared by the Legal Counsel (thrid-party), as part of control FR-EQ-15.

records.controlTitle: Stock-Based Compensation Journal Entry & Equity Rollforward
records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the Senior Manager of Accounting prepares the equity rollforward workbook and the related journals. The Accounting Director reviews the equity rollforward file and journals by agreeing the activity to supporting documentation (i.e., Board of Directors meeting minutes, exercise letters, etc.) to verify all activity has been accurately captured. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and comments throughout the file.

Monthly, the Legal Counsel (third-party) prepares the Capitalization Table that is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director. Evidence of review is via signoff, email and/or comments throughout the file. Enhancement for Abivax to consider: Consider adding a small amount of detail such as (we would include this in a 404(b) RCM but not 404(a). In the performance of the review, the Accounting Director verifies the following agreed back to the respective source documents: 1. Verifies the number of shares, the number of voting rights, exercises and the movements over the period. 2. Verifies the stock balance agrees to supporting schedules/documentation. 3. Spot checks formulas throughout to validate clerical accuracy. 4. Evidence of review is via signoff, email and/or comments throughout the file.

Quarterly, the Finance Manager prepares a memo for performance-based grants which assesses management’s determination of the probability and timing of achieving the performance-based criteria. Such conclusions are reflected in the current period compensation expense calculation and disclosures. The SVP Finance validates the conclusions are in line with the current business activities.

Quarterly, the Finance Manager performs the following administrator user access review procedures for Certent: 1. Reviews Certent User Access Reports to ensure user access is commensurate with user responsibilities and there is no conflicting access. 2. Compares the User Access Reports to listing of terminated users from HR to ensure that all terminated employees are listed as inactive and do not retain access to in scope systems past their termination date. 3. Reviews administrator access listings for appropriateness, as applicable. 4. If exceptions are identified, lookback procedures to evaluate the root cause and impact of the exception are performed and documented to ensure access is modified/removed following the corresponding formal process. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, comments, and email on the review.

Gap- While the company has various compensating controls, these controls are often manual do not fully address the risks that authorization, execution, processing, and reconciliation in the Equity processes are properly segregated and such , there remains an unmitigated risk that fraud, errors, or misstatement may occur. The Company should ensure the following (example non-key language below) for remediation: Duties are appropriately segregated: • Authorization and execution of equity transactions are segregated from recording of transactions • Equity processing is segregated from equity review • Equity reconciliation performance is segregated from reconciliation review

Gap- The Company does not perform or document an annual review of SOC 1 Type II reports for third-party and sub-service organizations. As a result, the adequacy and operating effectiveness of the third-party control environments are not evaluated. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that deficiencies or control failures at service organizations may go undetected, potentially resulting in errors or misstatements in the Company’s financial reporting. Below is the proposed control for remediation: SOC 1 reports are reviewed on an annual basis by the Company to ensure there are adequate controls at the third-party organizations and sub-service organizations and no significant deficiencies have been identified by the third party organization and sub-service organizations' external auditors. If there are deficiencies noted on the report, their significance is considered and evaluated. This review is evidenced via comments made on the Type II Report Workbook by the TBD by Management. For SOC 1 reports that are not through year-end, a bridge letter is obtained from the third party indicating that there are no material changes in the control environment through year-end.

Annually, the Head of FP&A prepares a financial forecast and a budget as part of the plan for the upcoming year which is reviewed by the CFO and CEO. The final budget is presented to the Board of Directors for approval. Evidence of approval is via the Board of Director's meeting minutes.

records.controlSummary: Only authorized users can make changes to the Chart of Account in Sage 100.
records.risk: Changes to chart of accounts and/or accounting system configuration are not accurate, complete, or properly authorized.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the GL Accountant/Senior GL Accountant extracts the current period’s chart of accounts from Sage 100 and reconciles it against the prior period’s chart to identify any additions or deletions. The updated chart is reviewed by the Accounting Director, who verifies that all changes were either pre-approved or appropriately justified. If discrepancies are identified, the GL Accountant/Senior GL Accountant updates Sage accordingly, following approval from the Accounting Director. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.
records.risk: Changes to chart of accounts and/or accounting system configuration are not accurate, complete, or properly authorized.

records.controlTitle: Journal entries are recorded to a closed period.
records.controlSummary: Sage 100 prevents users from posting journal entries to a closed period.
records.risk: Fictitious and/or fraudulent journal entries are posted to a closed period. Journal entries posted to closed periods result in transactions that are recorded in the inappropriate period.

records.controlSummary: Sage 100 prevents unbalanced journal entries from being posted.
records.risk: Journal entries do not balance.

records.controlSummary: Gap- The Company does not document materiality thresholds to guide the level of investigation and review performed for key financial analyses (e.g., Budget/Forecast to Actual Analysis, Balance Sheet Account Reconciliations, Flux Analysis, and Significant Transactions). As a result, there is no consistent or risk-based framework to determine which variances or transactions require further investigation and management review. As a result, there remains an unmitigated risk that material misstatements, unusual trends, or significant variances may not be identified, analyzed, or escalated on a timely basis. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Annually, the Head of FP&A establishes materiality thresholds for specific financial analyses performed throughout the year (Budget/Forecast to Actual Analysis, Balance Sheet Account Reconciliations, Flux Analysis, Significant Transactions) by defining a percentage and dollar value material threshold for variances noted. The results of the materiality thresholds are documented in a materiality memo, which is reviewed and approved by the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via comments throughout the memo and signoff or email signoff.
records.risk: Account balances are not reviewed at sufficient level of precision to identify errors and/or material misstatements. Significant variances are not investigated/reviewed and materially significant inaccuracies are included in the financial statements.

records.controlTitle: Cost centre allocation review (G&A and R&D split)
records.controlSummary: Gap- Although the Director of FP&A prepares the quarterly Income Statement Report by department to evaluate the R&D and G&A breakout and coding, the required independent review and approval by the Accounting Director is not being performed. The review is intended to include tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks; however, the activity is currently performed only by FP&A with no documented independent review or approval. As a result, there remains an unmitigated risk that errors in department allocation or coding may not be identified, increasing the possibility of inaccurate classification between R&D and G&A, leading to potential financial misstatement. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Senior Financial Analyst runs the Income Statement Report by department from Sage 100 to evaluate the R&D and G&A breakout and coding. The report is reviewed and approved by the Director of FP&A. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.
records.risk: Expenditures are not recorded/coded to the correct account

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the AP Team prepares a prepaid expense workbook, including supporting documentation (invoices and contracts). The workbook is submitted via email to the Accounting Director/Senior GL Accountant for review and approval. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.
records.risk: Prepaid balances are incomplete or inaccurate; the amortization period utilized to amortize prepaids is inaccurate, resulting in inaccurate amortization expense.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the Senior GL accountant prepares the G&A accruals and the Head of FP&A prepares the R&D accruals, and the Accounting Director reviews and approves the accruals greater than [$XX amount as defined by Management]. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.
records.risk: The Company does not know it has an obligation and therefore does not record a liability. Liabilities are not recognized in the correct period, resulting in a misstatement.
records.preparer: Head of FP&A Senior GL Accountant

records.controlTitle: Realized Gains and losses calculation and review
records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the unrealized foreign exchange gains and losses are calculated by the GL Accountant. The calculation is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director before posting via manual journal entries. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the workbook. Enhancement - Due to the identified material weakness in Sage 100, the Company cannot fully rely on automated calculations without independent validation. In this way, there is no detective control in place to review or validate that the exchange rates imported into Sage 100 are accurate, complete, and consistent with the official rates published by the France Central Bank, neither to verify that the realized gain/loss amounts calculated by Sage 100 are accurate and consistent with the underlying transaction and exchange rate data. Below is the proposed control for remediation: • REALIZED: Quarterly, the GL Accountant reviews the foreign currency exchange rates automatically imported from the France Central Bank into Sage 100 to verify that the rates were correctly imported. The GL Accountant spot checks the exchange rates in Sage 100 to the official rates published by the France Central Bank. Also, the GL Accountant performs a review of realized foreign currency gains and losses automatically calculated by Sage 100, recalculating the realized gain or loss using the appropriate exchange rates to confirm accuracy. • UNREALIZED: The unrealized gains and losses are calculated by the Senior GL Accountant. The calculation is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director before posting via manual journal entries. Evidence of review is retained via screenshots, comparison logs, and sign-off within the workbook.
records.risk: Realized and unrealized foreign currency is not appropriately calculated.
records.preparer: REALIZED: GL Accountant UNREALIZED: Senior GL Accountant

records.controlTitle: Automated Import of Foreign Currency Exchange Rates
records.controlSummary: Foreign currency rates are automatically imported from France Central Bank to Sage 100 on a daily basis. Note: Sage 100 has an identified material weakness, and therefore, reliance cannot be placed solely on automated system processes. A mitigating detective control has been implemented separately to independently verify the accuracy and completeness of the foreign currency rates imported into Sage 100.
records.risk: Transaction in foreign currency not properly valued (using wrong exchange rates).

records.controlTitle: Realized gain and losses are automatically calculated
records.controlSummary: Sage 100 automatically calculates realized gains and losses during the accounting close process. Note: Sage 100 has an identified material weakness, and therefore, reliance cannot be placed solely on automated system processes. A mitigating detective control has been implemented separately to independently verify the accuracy and completeness of the foreign currency rates imported into Sage 100.
records.risk: Realized and unrealized foreign currency is not appropriately calculated.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the GL accountant proceeds to BS and P&L reconciliation between intercompany accounts for all consolidated entities. Reconciliation is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director. Any differences are investigated and resolved. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.
records.risk: Intercompany transactions are not accurate nor complete.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, all balance sheet accounts (US, FR) are reconciled based on French GAAP. The Accounting Director (FR) or SVP Finance (US) reviews and approves the reconciliations and verifies that the general ledger balance agrees to supporting detail and any all variances are investigated, resolved and documented within the reconciliation. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.
records.risk: Account reconciliations are not reviewed (or reviewed, but not at a high level of precision). Account balances are not complete and accurate.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the GL Accountant/Accounting Director sends the most recent Balance Sheet and P&L accounts for the consolidated components (FR and US) to the third-party IFRS service provider via a shared file. The provider returns an updated IFRS mapping file. GAP - There is no internal review of the mapping performed by the third party. Below is the proposed control for remediation: The Accounting Director reviews the mapping to ensure that all French GAAP accounts are accurately mapped to IFRS, as evidenced via sign-off via email.
records.risk: Incorrect or Incomplete Mapping of French GAAP Accounts to IFRS.
records.system: Sage 100 (for Abivax SA and Abivax LLC B/S)
records.remediationPlan: Gap - There is no internal review of the mapping performed by the third party.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the third-party provider converts the financial statements of entities whose reporting currency is not the euro for consolidation purposes. The workbook is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director. Evidence of review the conversion file is completed as part of control FR-FSCP-21 (consolidation).
records.risk: Conversion of consolidated financial statements is not accurate.

records.controlSummary: Gap - The Company reviews the work of the third-party however, there is no formal evidence retained that supports the adequacy of management reviews procedures and oversight of third parties. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that the third-party has errors in the file or does not have institutional knowledge to include all relevant transactions. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the third-party provider performs the manual consolidation of the LLC (US) and SA (FR) entities. The consolidation includes adjustments for intercompany eliminations, and alignment IFRS adjustments. The workbook is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director and the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.
records.risk: Consolidated Financial Statement are not accurate nor complete.
records.remediationPlan: Gap - Formal evidence of review is not retained.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the balance sheet and income statement flux analyses are prepared by the Head of FP&A, to identify unexpected and/or unusual activity. Significant variances (as defined by Management) are investigated, resolved, and documented within the analyses by the Head of FP&A. The flux analyses are reviewed and approved by the SVP Finance. GAP - No evidence of review of the flux analysis is retained. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes and sign-off within the consolidation workbooks.
records.risk: Errors or anomalies in financial statements are not identified and investigated/resolved, resulting in misstatements. Flux analyses are not reviewed at a level of precision to identify material errors and/or misstatements.
records.remediationPlan: Gap- evidence of review to be retained

records.controlSummary: Gap - Currently, the Company reviews the work of the third party however, there is no formal evidence retained that supports the adequacy of management reviews procedures and oversight of third parties. As such, it remains an unmitigated risk that the third party has errors in the file or does not have institutional knowledge to include all relevant transaction. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the third party provider prepares the Statement of Cash Flows, which is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director and SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via comments/tickmarks throughout the file, and signoff on the Statement of Cash Flows and supporting cash flow tie out.
records.risk: Balances are not accurately presented in the statement of cash flows. Transactions presented in the statement of cash flows are misclassified between operating, investing, and financing activities.
records.remediationPlan: Gap - Formal evidence of review is not retained.

records.controlSummary: Gap - While the Company reviews the work of the third party, there is no formal evidence retained that supports the adequacy of management reviews procedures and oversight of third parties. As such, it remains an unmitigated risk that the third party has errors in the consolidation file or does not have institutional knowledge to include all relevant transaction. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Accounting Director performs a review of the Consolidated FS prepared by the third party to validate the accuracy and completeness of the Equity movements for the period by reconciliating the individual vs the consolidated statements of changes in equity. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.
records.risk: Balances are not appropriately or accurately presented in the statement of cash flows. Transactions presented in the statement of cash flows are misclassified between operating, investing, and financing activities.
records.remediationPlan: Gap - Formal evidence of review is not retained.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the third-party provider prepares and populates the Financial Statements workbook, which includes consolidated financial data for the reporting period. The Accounting Director performs a detailed review of the workbook to verify the accuracy of the data, consistency with supporting documentation, and validating that numbers are accurate. The SVP Finance performs a secondary review to validate the completeness and accuracy of the financial statements. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks and Workiva. Enhancement: Management to consider and implement a formal review process in 2026.
records.risk: Consolidated Financial Statement are not accurate nor complete.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, prior to the issuance of the 6-K/20-F, the Disclosure Committee independently reviews the financial statements and all footnote disclosures. Evidence of review is maintained within Workiva and via a signed disclosure checklist.
records.risk: Those charged with governance do not have sufficient oversight over the organizational activities, resulting in oversight gaps.

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the basic and diluted EPS calculations are reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director, prior to issuance of the financial statements. Evidence of review is via signoff in the workbook, email correspondence and/or comments throughout the file.
records.risk: EPS is calculated and reported inaccurately.

records.controlSummary: Gap- While the Company performs a tie-out of the Form 20-F and Form 6-K to ensure the accuracy of financial information and related disclosures, the process is not formally documented, and evidence of review is not consistently retained. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that tie-out procedures and related management reviews may not be performed consistently across reporting periods, resulting in potential errors, omissions, or misstatements in the financial statements and footnote disclosures. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Finance Manager performs a tie out of the 20-F/6-K including balance sheet, income statement, statement of stockholders’ equity, statement of cash flows, and footnote disclosures, as evidenced by tickmarks and review notes within the work file. The tie-out is reviewed and approved by the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via signoff on the financial statements and tickmarks/notations throughout the report.
records.risk: The financial statements are not presented in accordance with the prescribed guidance. Balances are not appropriately or accurately presented in the statement of cash flows. The presented events, transactions, management assertions, and other matters may not have occurred or pertain to the Company.
records.remediationPlan: Gap - Formal evidence of review is not retained.

records.controlSummary: Enhancement - As the Company filler status changes, a disclosure checklist will be required at each filling. Currently, the Company prepares and reviews the IFRS Disclosure Checklist on an annual basis as part of the year-end reporting process, but will be required to complete this quarterly. Below is the proposed control for enhancement: Quarterly, the IFRS Disclosure Checklist is prepared by the Finance Manager and reviewed by the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via signoff on the Disclosure Checklist.
records.risk: Financial statement disclosures are not in compliance with IFRS requirements. Required disclosures included in the financial statements are incomplete and/or inaccurate.
records.remediationPlan: Enhancement - Control frequency to be adjusted to quarterly as filer status changes.

records.controlSummary: The Group Treasurer prepares and the SVP Finance reviews the Going Concern memo. No evidence of the SVP review is retained. Gap- As the Company transitions to quarterly filings, a formal going concern assessment process needs to be established. While the Company prepares cash flow projections on a periodic basis, a formal going concern assessment is not performed or documented quarterly. Specifically, a Going Concern Memo is not consistently prepared to evaluate the reasonableness of key assumptions. Also, it is recommended that the evidence of review by the CFO to be formally retained. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that indicators of potential liquidity or solvency issues may not be identified or appropriately disclosed on a timely basis, potentially resulting in incomplete or inaccurate financial statement disclosures. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Group Treasurer prepares the Cash Flows Projection (which includes the Cash Forecast and cash burn), which is used to document the assessment of the Company's ability to continue as a going concern. The SVP Finance reviews the supporting documentation. If necessary, the Going Concern Memo is prepared to evaluate the reasonableness of the assumptions and the conclusion, by the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is documented via signoff on the final memo, by SVP Finance.
records.risk: The entity is not able to continue as a going concern. Financial statement disclosures are not in compliance with IFRS requirements.
records.remediationPlan: Gap- Document the review of the CFO and document the going concern memo

records.controlSummary: Enhancement - As the Company transitions to quarterly filings as a US filler (not a FPI), this control should be implemented to ensure that executive certifications are obtained on a quarterly basis. Below is the proposed control for enhancement: Quarterly, after approval by the Audit Committee to publish the financial statements to the SEC, the CEO and CFO sign the 302 & 404 certifications to evidence approval of the form 10-Q/10-K.
records.risk: Management does not evaluate impacts to the risk assessment as a result of changes to internal controls. Management is not aware of/does not disclose changes to internal controls. Financial statement disclosures are not in compliance with US GAAP requirements.
records.remediationPlan: Enhancement - Control frequency to be adjusted to quarterly as filer status changes.

records.controlSummary: Gap- While the Company performs an annual Director and Officer (“D&O”) Questionnaire process to confirm independence of Board and Executive members, the procedures surrounding distribution, review, and retention of the completed questionnaires are not formally documented. Additionally, evidence of review by the General Legal Counsel is not consistently retained. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that the Company may be unable to demonstrate compliance with independence assessment requirements or support the completeness and accuracy of disclosures related to Board and Executive independence. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Annually, all Board of Director and Executive Officer members complete the Director and Officer ("D&O") Questionnaire to confirm independence. The results of the Director and Officer questionnaires are reviewed by the general legal counsel and the SVP Finance or Designee. To be implemented by Abivax in 2026
records.risk: Board of Directors and Executive Officers are not independent in fact and appearance. Related Parties are not identified and disclosed.
records.reviewer: Legal Counsel SVP Finance or Designee
records.remediationPlan: Gap- Document and retain evidence of the questionnaires and responses.

records.controlSummary: Gap - There is no process for Directors and Officers to recertify their independence and related parties. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that Directors and Officers are not independent and the Company does not identify all related parties that require disclosure. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Annually, the General Legal Counsel sends the Directors and Officers an independence certification via email, to update any changes from their D&O Questionnaire responses to their independence, and disclose any known related party transactions that occurred during the period. The SVP Finance (or designee) reviews the results of the recertifications and updates the related party listing, as necessary.
records.risk: Board of Directors and Executive Officers are not independent in fact and appearance. Related Parties are not identified and disclosed.
records.reviewer: Legal Counsel SVP Finance or Designee

records.controlSummary: Gap - Management does not currently have a formal process in place to identify and/or monitor related party transactions. Public companies are required to disclose material related party transactions (i.e., nature, type and dollar amount of transactions) that occur during the reporting period. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that there are related party transactions that the Company is unaware of resulting in the financial statement disclosures to be incomplete and/or inaccurate. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Senior GL Accountant reviews the Related Party Relationships Listing against the Sage 100 Transactions Report for the period to determine if any related party transactions occurred that require disclosure. The analysis is provided to the SVP Finance or Accounting Director for review and approval. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes and sign-off within the workbooks.
records.risk: Related party transactions occur that the Company is not aware of, resulting in incomplete and/or inaccurate disclosures.

records.controlSummary: Gap - The Company does not have a formalized control in place to ensure that all significant or non-routine transactions are appropriately reviewed and approved by the Board of Directors prior to execution. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that material transactions could be undertaken without proper oversight or authorization, potentially resulting in non-compliance with corporate governance requirements and misstatements in the financial statements. Below is the proposed control for remediation: All significant/non-routine transactions are prepared by the Finance Manager and/or the Accounting Director and approved by the Board of Directors. The Company defines the following to be considered significant/non-routine transactions to be (and others, at the BOD's discretion): -Debt issuance -Equity transactions -Related party transactions -At-the-market offerings -Acquisitions/acquisition valuations -Common-stock issuances - Warrant Issuances -Dividend payments -Amendments to articles of incorporation -Other transactions at the discretion of the Board of Directors Evidence of approval is via Board of Directors meeting minutes or consent as applicable related to the significant non-routine transaction(s).
records.risk: Transactions are not authorized, resulting in insufficient oversight from the Board or those delegated with authorization responsibilities. The Company does not assess significant, complex, or non-routine transactions that may have accounting and/or reporting implications

records.controlSummary: Gap - The Company does not have a formalized process in place to ensure that significant or non-standard transactions are subject to appropriate technical accounting analysis and management review prior to recording. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that complex or unusual transactions may not be accounted for in accordance with IFRS, potentially resulting in misstatements in the financial statements or incomplete disclosures. Below is the proposed control for remediation: As needed, for significant (as defined by the company) and non-standard transactions, a Third-Party Consultant, Technical Accountant or similar prepares a technical analysis and memo that details the nature of the transaction and the potential accounting implications. The analysis and memo are reviewed and approved by the SVP Finance and/or CFO prior to the recording of the transaction. Evidence of review is performed via email approval, comments throughout the memo, and physical or electronic sign-off on the memo.
records.risk: The Company does not assess significant, complex, or non-routine transactions that may have accounting and/or reporting implications. Significant, non-routine transactions or legal contingencies are not properly accounted for and reported.

records.controlSummary: Gap- The Company does not have a formalized process in place to monitor, evaluate and document the impact of new or recently issued accounting pronouncements (ASUs) on the financial statements and related disclosures. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that changes in accounting guidance may not be identified or implemented timely, potentially resulting in non-compliance with IFRS reporting requirements and incomplete or inaccurate financial statement disclosures. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Third Party Provider evaluates any new or recently issued accounting pronouncements/ASUs to verify the financial statement disclosures are in line with the latest reporting requirements. The Accounting Director and/or SVP Finance reviews and approves the ASU workbook. Evidence of review is denoted in the Company's ASU workbook or via email correspondence and/or signoff on the GAAP Checklist. To be implemented by Abivax in 2026
records.risk: Financial statement balances and disclosures do not properly reflect GAAP, SEC, or recently issued accounting pronouncements. Financial statement disclosures are not in compliance with IFRS requirements.

records.controlSummary: Gap- The quarterly user access review is not being performed or documented as designed. As a result, key procedures, including validating user access appropriateness, confirming timely removal of terminated employees, and reviewing administrator access, are not executed or evidenced. These activities are manual and inconsistent, and therefore there remains an unmitigated risk unauthorized, inappropriate, or conflicting access within systems may go undetected. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the [Role - Finance] performs the following administrator user access review procedures for Sage 100: 1. Reviews Sage 100 User Access Reports to ensure user access is commensurate with user responsibilities and there is no conflicting access. 2. Compares the User Access Reports to listing of terminated users from HR to ensure that all terminated employees are listed as inactive and do not retain access to in scope systems past their termination date. 3. Reviews administrator access listings for appropriateness, as applicable. 4. If exceptions are identified, lookback procedures to evaluate the root cause and impact of the exception are performed and documented to ensure access is modified/removed following the corresponding formal process. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, comments, and email on the review. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation
records.risk: Unauthorized or inappropriate additions or modifications are made to the master file.

records.controlSummary: Gap - While the company has various compensating controls, these controls are often manual and do not fully address the risks that authorization, execution, processing, and reconciliation in the FSCP processes are properly segregated and as such, there remains an unmitigated risk that fraud, errors, or misstatement may occur. The Company should ensure the following (example non-key language below) for remediation: Duties are appropriately segregated: • Preparation and review of journal entries are segregated. • Posting of journal entries is segregated from their preparation and review. • Preparation of account reconciliations is segregated from reconciliation review. • Preparation of financial statements and management reports is segregated from their review and approval. • Overall reconciliation performance is segregated from reconciliation review.
records.risk: Incompatible duties related to processing are not appropriately segregated.
records.remediationPlan: Gap - Segregation of Duties Conflicts Identified.

records.controlSummary: Gap- The Company does not perform or document an annual review of SOC 1 Type II reports for third-party and sub-service organizations. As a result, the adequacy and operating effectiveness of the third-party control environments are not evaluated. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that deficiencies or control failures at service organizations may go undetected, potentially resulting in errors or misstatements in the Company’s financial reporting. Below is the proposed control for remediation: SOC 1 reports are reviewed on an annual basis by the Company to ensure there are adequate controls at the third-party organizations and sub-service organizations and no significant deficiencies have been identified by the third party organization and sub-service organizations' external auditors. If there are deficiencies noted on the report, their significance is considered and evaluated. This review is evidenced via comments made on the Type II Report Workbook by the TBD by Management. For SOC 1 reports that are not through year-end, a bridge letter is obtained from the third party indicating that there are no material changes in the control environment through year-end.
records.risk: Complementary user entity controls (CEUCs) per the service auditor's SOC-1 report are implemented inaccurately.

records.controlSummary: As needed, upon signing of the Offer Letter, new hire data is entered into ADP by the VP HR. Evidence of the input with supporting screenshots are reviewed and approved by the CPO to confirm accuracy of the input. Evidence of review is via email.

Monthly, before the payment run, the VP HR prepares an excel report of all employee masterdata changes (new or changes to existing employees) which is reviewed and approved by the CPO via email. As part of the review, the CPO reviews all employee changes to supporting screenshots from ADP Decidium, provided by the VP HR. Evidence of review by the CPO is via email.

records.controlTitle: Annual Bonus and Merit Validation and Review
records.controlSummary: Annually, once the merit increases and bonus payout calculations for employees are approved by the Board, the VP HR enters the new, or changes to, salaries into ADP. The VP HR then sends an email with screenshots evidencing the updates made in ADP which is reviewed and approved by the Chief People officer, as evidenced by email.

All timesheets for eligible employees are reviewed and approved by the employee's supervisor within the Lucca system. The R&D staff time allocation is reviewed by the Accounting Director and data submitted with the annual tax return.

records.controlTitle: Authorisation and release of wire payment
records.controlSummary: Monthly, prior to approving the payment of payroll, the VP HR emails and the Accounting Director reviews the current and prior month gross payroll, and explanations for differences. A confirmation email of the approval is sent to the VP HR by the Accounting Director, which confirms the wire transfer has been initiated. Also refer to FR-HtR-11 for reviews performed after the payment is processed. Refer to the Treasury and PtP Processes for creation and releases of the wire.

Monthly, after the payment processing and the ADP reports are released, the VP HR submits the payroll variable file (including bonuses, commissions, adjustments, extra paid hours) via email to the Chief People Officer. The file is reviewed and approved by the Chief People Officer as evidenced via email.

Monthly, the payroll journal entry is prepared by the Senior Manager of Accounting and reviewed by the Accounting Director, who compares journals against supporting documentation (for example pre-process payroll register, ADP invoice and evidence of approval of journal). Evidence of review is via email.

Quarterly, the Senior Manager of Accounting prepares the Bonus accrual calculation and related journal entry. The accrual calculation is reviewed and approved by the VP HR against the contracts (percentage of bonus). Final review is performed by the Accounting Director, as evidenced via tickmarks and notes within the workbook or sign-off via email.

Quarterly, the Senior Manager of Accounting prepares the vacation accrual calculation and related journal entry. The vacation accrual calculation is reviewed and approved by the Accounting Director, as evidenced via tickmarks and notes within the workbook or sign-off via email.

Quarterly the Senior Manager of Accounting prepares and the Accounting Director reviews and approves the payroll account reconciliations, to verify that the general ledger balances agree to supporting documentation and payroll account balances are complete and accurate. Significant variances (as defined by Management) are investigated, evidenced through comments on the reconciliation. The Accounting Director review is evidenced via email approval. Note that this control (BS reconciliations) will remain in each process in year one, but consolidated as one control and moved to financial statement close (FSCP) in year two.

Gap- The quarterly ADP user access review is not being performed or documented as designed. As a result, key procedures, including validating user access appropriateness, confirming timely removal of terminated employees, and reviewing administrator access, are not executed or evidenced. These activities are manual and inconsistent, and therefore there remains an unmitigated risk unauthorized, inappropriate, or conflicting access within ADP may go undetected. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the [Role - Finance] performs the following administrator user access review procedures for ADP: 1. Reviews ADP User Access Reports to ensure user access is commensurate with user responsibilities and there is no conflicting access. 2. Compares the User Access Reports to listing of terminated users from HR to ensure that all terminated employees are listed as inactive and do not retain access to in scope systems past their termination date. 3. Reviews administrator access listings for appropriateness, as applicable. 4. If exceptions are identified, lookback procedures to evaluate the root cause and impact of the exception are performed and documented to ensure access is modified/removed following the corresponding formal process. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, comments, and email on the review.

Gap- While the company has various compensating controls, these controls are often manual do not fully address the risks that authorization, execution, processing, and reconciliation in the HR / Payroll processes are properly segregated and such , there remains an unmitigated risk that fraud, errors, or misstatement may occur. The Company should ensure the following (example non-key language below) for remediation: Duties are appropriately segregated: • Authorization and execution of payroll transactions are segregated from recording of transactions • Payroll processing is segregated from payroll review • Payroll reconciliation performance is segregated from reconciliation review

Gap- The Company does not perform or document an annual review of SOC 1 Type II reports for third-party and sub-service organizations. As a result, the adequacy and operating effectiveness of the third-party control environments are not evaluated. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that deficiencies or control failures at service organizations may go undetected, potentially resulting in errors or misstatements in the Company’s financial reporting. Below is the proposed control for remediation: SOC 1 reports are reviewed on an annual basis by the Company to ensure there are adequate controls at the third-party organizations and sub-service organizations and no significant deficiencies have been identified by the third party organization and sub-service organizations' external auditors. If there are deficiencies noted on the report, their significance is considered and evaluated. This review is evidenced via comments made on the Type II Report Workbook by the TBD by Management. For SOC 1 reports that are not through year-end, a bridge letter is obtained from the third party indicating that there are no material changes in the control environment through year-end.

Annually, the SVP Finance reviews the Delegation of Authority Policy. As needed, any changes to the Delegation of Authority Policy are approved by the SVP Finance. Evidence of approval is via sign-off.
records.risk: A lack of clear guidance on approval authorization results in insufficient oversight by those delegated with authorization responsibilities over transactions that may require special accounting treatment or disclosure.

records.controlSummary: Gap- While a compensating controls exist within the procure-to-pay process, there is currently no formal, documented review of additions, deletions, or modifications to vendor master data within Sage. Changes to the vendor master file are not consistently reviewed or approved by the Senior GL accountant and evidence of such review is retained via email. Because these controls are manual and not operating as designed within the ERP, they do not sufficiently mitigate the risk that unauthorized, inaccurate, or inappropriate vendor information may be created or modified. Below is the proposed control for remediation: All changes to the vendor data in Sage (i.e. additions/delete/modifications) are reviewed and approved by the Senior GL Accountant. Evidence of review is via email. Also refer to FR-PTP-03 for data validation checks by Trustpair. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation, as no functionality in Sage 100 to produce reporting.
records.risk: Unauthorized or fictitious changes are made to vendor master data, leading to fraudulent or unauthorized disbursements.

records.controlSummary: As required, changes to the vendor data in Sage (additions/modifications) are validated for accuracy and legitimacy by the AP Accountant against third-party Trustpair reports, which automatically verifies vendors information from Sage 100 twice a day. The AP Accountant approves the vendor in Sage as evidence of review.
records.risk: Vendor data is not properly validated, resulting in insufficient oversight over the accuracy and legitimacy of vendor master data. Unauthorized or fictitious changes are made to vendor master data, leading to fraudulent or unauthorized disbursements.

records.controlSummary: All new purchase orders are initiated in DocuShare by the requestor and manually routed for approval based on a pre-defined drop down approval list configured within the system. Gap- Although purchase orders are initiated in DocuShare and routed for approval based on a predefined drop-down approval list, the system does not prevent users from approving their own purchase orders, even when they are included in the DOA approval hierarchy. While instances of self-approval can be identified after the fact, the lack of a system-enforced segregation of duties results in an unmitigated risk that POs may be self-approved without appropriate oversight. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation.
records.risk: Purchase Orders are not authorized, resulting in insufficient oversight from those delegated with authorization responsibilities. There are financial obligations the Company is unaware of, resulting in a misstatement in expenses.

records.controlSummary: Gap- The Company does not currently perform a periodic review of open orders to identify items that have remained outstanding beyond a defined threshold. As a result, there is no process in place to ensure that long-outstanding orders are timely investigated and resolved. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Monthly, the AP Accountant, runs an exception report for all open orders from DocuShare. All open orders outstanding for over 2 months are investigated and resolved. The exception workbook is reviewed and approved by the Senior GL accountant/Accounting Director. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation. No functionality in DocuShare to produce reporting.
records.risk: Vendor invoices are recorded untimely, inaccurately, or incompletely.

records.controlSummary: a) Purchase Order-based invoice: A manual 2-way match (PO/Invoice) is performed by the Accounts Payable. Discrepancies [based on a percentage and amount, as defined by Management are investigated and resolved with the operations team prior to manual entry into the DocuShare system. Gap- There is currently no automated two-way match in DocuShare and the manual 2-way match may result in errors; therefore, there remains an unmitigated risk that discrepancies may go undetected. There is an increased risk that inaccurate, duplicate, or unauthorized invoices may be processed or paid, resulting in potential financial misstatement, improper payment, or fraud. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation. b) Non-Purchase Order-based Invoice are approved in accordance with an approved delegation of authority within DocuShare.
records.risk: Unauthorized invoices are processed, resulting in those delegated with authorization responsibilities having insufficient oversight. Below is the proposed control for remediation: A monthly reconciliation is performed between DocuShare and Sage to ensure that payments are processed only if invoices are approved in DocuShare.
records.risk: Unauthorized invoices are processed, resulting in those delegated with authorization responsibilities having insufficient oversight.
