records.controlSummary: Gap- The Company does not currently perform a formal Search for Unrecorded Liabilities. Key steps, such as evaluating subsequent disbursements against the defined thresholds and documenting conclusions, are not reliably formalized. In this way, there is an unmitigated risk that unrecorded liabilities may not be identified or accrued in the proper period. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the AP Accountant prepares the Search for Unrecorded Liabilities Analysis to determine if subsequent disbursements / invoices greater than [based on a percentage and amount, as defined by Management] were properly included or excluded from the quarterly accruals. The final Search for Unrecorded Liabilities Analysis is reviewed and approved by the Senior GL Accountant and Director of FP&A. Evidence of review is via comments throughout the file, tickmarks, sign-off and/or email communication. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation.
records.risk: The Company does not know it has an obligation and therefore does not record a liability. Liabilities are not recognized in the correct period, resulting in a misstatement.
records.reviewer: Senior GL Accountant and Director of FP&A

records.controlSummary: CFGI COMMENT: We understand that this was performed once off in 2025, however this is a key control that should be performed annually going forward. Annually, the AP Accountant reviews Vendor Master file to identify any duplicates or inactive vendors. Any modifications as a result of this review are identified reviewed by the Senior GL accountant/Accounting Director and approved via email before being updated in Sage.
records.risk: Purchases are performed using inaccurate, fraudulent or inappropriate suppliers data Fictious/Unauthorized creations/modifications made to the supplier master file data

records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the Senior GL Accountant prepares the accounts payable account reconciliations, to verify that the general ledger balances agree to supporting documentation and accounts payable account balances are complete and accurate. As part of the reconciliation, the Senior GL Accountant reviews the AP Aging Report. Significant variances (as defined by Management) are investigated. The Accounting Director reviews and approves the reconciliation as evidenced via email approval, comments throughout the spreadsheet, and physical or electronic sign-off on the spreadsheet.
records.risk: Accounts Payable account balances are not complete and accurate. Account reconciliations are not reviewed (or reviewed, but not at a high level of precision)

records.controlSummary: Gap- While the company has various compensating controls, these controls are often manual do not fully address the risks that authorization, execution, processing, and reconciliation in the PtP processes are properly segregated and such , there remains an unmitigated risk that fraud, errors, or misstatement may occur. Below is the proposed control for remediation: The Company should ensure the following (example non-key language below) for remediation: Duties are appropriately segregated: • Authorization and execution of payment transactions are segregated from recording of transactions • Payment and employee expense reimbursement processing is segregated from payment and expense review. • Invoice processing is segregated from invoice review • Payment processing is segregated from payment review
records.risk: Incompatible duties related to processing are not appropriately segregated

records.controlSummary: Gap- The quarterly Sage user access review is not being performed or documented as designed. As a result, key procedures, including validating user access appropriateness, confirming timely removal of terminated employees, and reviewing administrator access, are not executed or evidenced. These activities are manual and inconsistent, and therefore there remains an unmitigated risk unauthorized, inappropriate, or conflicting access within Sage may go undetected. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the GL Accountant performs the following administrator user access review procedures for Sage: 1. Reviews Sage User Access Reports to ensure user access is commensurate with user responsibilities and there is no conflicting access. 2. Compares the User Access Reports to listing of terminated users from HR to ensure that all terminated employees are listed as inactive and do not retain access to in scope systems past their termination date. 3. Reviews administrator access listings for appropriateness, as applicable. 4. If exceptions are identified, lookback procedures to evaluate the root cause and impact of the exception are performed and documented to ensure access is modified/removed following the corresponding formal process. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, comments, and email on the review. To be implemented as part of the new ERP implementation.
records.risk: Application end-users and other high-risk/powerful accounts (e.g., super-user, system administrators, database administrators etc.) bypass systems-enforced authorization and segregation of duties controls, resulting in inappropriate transactions, unauthorized activities, or improper direct changes being made to underlying transaction records or master data

records.controlSummary: Control prior to the filing of the VAT : Monthly, the AP Accountant (Juliette or Fatma) prepares and the GL Accountant (Denis) reviews and approves the VAT Calculation File using VAT GL extractions from Sage and the CA3 (VAT return). Any discrepancies identified are corrected in the subsequent VAT declaration. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.

Control of the posting after the filing : Monthly, the AP Accountant records the VAT amounts from the validated VAT Calculation File into Sage 100 through a manual journal entry based on recorded invoices. The GL Accountant reviews and verifies that the booked VAT amounts in Sage 100 align with the calculated figures in the VAT Calculation File and the VAT declaration.

Annually, the tax return package is prepared by the third party provider (In Extenso). Once the package is finalized, the Senior Accounting Manager performs a detailed review of the workbook to verify the data inputs with supporting documentation. The Accounting Director performs a secondary review to validate the completeness and accuracy of the tax return. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks. Additionally, KPMG Tax Lawyers prepare the taxable income calculation, which is reviewed by Accounting Manager and Accounting Director. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.

Quaterly the GL Accountant (Denis J) identifies and documents external expenses related to research activities (GL account 604) in an Excel workbook, reviewed by the Accounting Director. Personnel expenses are also reviewed quarterly by Accounting Director to confirm the eligibility of staff (e.g., role, qualifications) and the accuracy of reported research hours as evidenced by color coded tickmarks and comments within the Excel file. At year-end, the Accounting Director consolidates all supporting information and send it to third party (KPMG) who provides feedback on the full year calculation and that elements taken into account are effectively eligible to CIR calculation. KPMG also send information in case of a change in the CIR calculation by tax authorities

Annually, the General Ledger Accountant submits the CIR (Crédit d’Impôt Recherche) declaration (Form 2069 and annexes) on the French government portal (as part of the tax filing). A reconciliation is performed between the CIR calculation file, the official declaration, and related accounting entries. The Accounting Director reviews and validates the declared CIR amount for consistency with the approved calculation. Any discrepancies are investigated and resolved prior to submission. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.

records.controlTitle: Other applicable taxes calculation / declaration (CFE / Apprenticeship / Alternance / Handicap)
records.controlSummary: Quarterly, the external service provider prepares the CFE, Apprenticeship and Alternance tax declarations. The Senior accountant reviews and reconciles the calculated amounts to the declared figures and corresponding booking entries in Sage. The Accounting Director reviews to ensure all applicable taxes are accurately calculated and properly recorded. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.

As needed, The Senior Accountant Finance and Accounting Director review professional literature and guidance from 3rd parties to make updates to tax estimates as a result of changes to tax laws, regulation, industry developments, or ASC guidance. On an annual basis, they participate in a seminar or webinar hosted by one of the Big Four firms or by the Francis Lefebvre law firm, focusing on the latest tax developments Evidence of review should include newsletters from third-party providers, retention of these newsletters, and proof of attendance at the seminar or webinar, together with any supporting materials provided.

All opening and closing of bank accounts are approved by the CFO or SVP Finance via email. Approval, with the related bank documents, including the Délégation des pouvoirs bancaires Contract are stored in the Treasury share folder.

Annually and as needed, bank account signature authorizations are reviewed by the Treasury Director against the approved bank signatory list to validate that all signatories are for active, authorized employees. Additionally, should a triggering event occur (e.g. termination, promotion), the SVP Finance and the CFO evaluates whether there are any resulting changes to the bank account signatories. Evidence of review/approval is documented via email and stored in the Treasury share folder.

The Company maintains an investment policy, and at least annually or as needed, any modifications to the policy are reviewed by the SVP Finance and approved by the Board of Directors as evidenced via meeting minutes, written consent or sign off.

As needed, the Treasury Director identifies appropriate investment opportunities for the Company and shares recommendations with the SVP Finance and CFO for validation and approval prior to execution. In addition, any subsequent adjustments or modifications to existing investments exceeding a defined monetary threshold [based on a percentage and amount of the total investment portfolio, as defined by Management, whichever is lower] must be reviewed and approved by the SVP Finance and CFO before being executed. Evidence of approval is retained via email correspondence or formal sign-off on the investment summary. All related postings are recorded through manual journal entries and appropriately supported.

As needed, debt agreements are presented by the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) to the Board of Directors for approval, as documented in meeting minutes and signed by both the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and the Managing Director.

Quarterly, the Treasury Director prepares the loan amortization schedule, which is reviewed by the GL Accountant for accuracy and completeness, as part of the reconciliation control FR-TR-10 . The review is evidenced through notes, tickmarks, and sign-off within the amortization schedule workbook. Additionally, the SVP Finance performs and documents a review of the fair value calculation prepared by the third party to assess the reasonableness of key assumptions, inputs, and results. Evidence of this review is retained via sign-off on the fair value report and/or supporting review documentation. The approved amortization schedule and validated fair value analysis are used by the Senior Accountant to post the corresponding journal entries.

As needed, modifications to loan agreements are approved by the Board of Directors or Designee, as defined by the DoA policy. Evidence of approval is documented in meeting minutes, written resolution or email confirmations and signoff on the loan agreements.

Gap - There is no formally defined process or assigned ownership for the preparation and review of the Bank Covenant Schedule. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that covenant calculations or related disclosures may be incomplete, inaccurate, or not reviewed timely, potentially resulting in noncompliance with debt agreements, misclassification of debt, or incomplete disclosures in the financial statements. Below is the proposed control for remediation: On a quarterly basis, the [Role - TBD by Management] prepares and maintains the Bank Covenant Schedule (excel) which captures all negotiated obligations and covenants. The [Role - TBD by Management] reviews and approves the analysis together with any disclosure.

Monthly, the Accountant prepares the bank account reconciliations, to verify that the general ledger balances agree to supporting documentation (i.e., bank statements) and account balances are complete and accurate. Significant variances (as defined by Management) are investigated and resolved. The Accounting Director reviews and approves the reconciliation as evidenced via email approval and comments throughout the spreadsheet.

Quarterly, the GL Accountant prepares the loans account reconciliations, to verify that the general ledger balances agree to supporting documentation (i.e., bank statements, loans calculations) and account balances are complete and accurate. Significant variances (as defined by Management) are investigated and resolved. The Accounting Director reviews and approves the reconciliation as evidenced via email approval and comments throughout the spreadsheet.

Reviews Agicap User Access Reports to ensure user access is commensurate with user responsibilities and there is no conflicting access. 2. Compares the User Access Reports to listing of terminated users from HR to ensure that all terminated employees are listed as inactive and do not retain access to in scope systems past their termination date. 3. Gap - While the company has various compensating controls, these controls are often manual do not fully address the risks that authorization, execution, processing, and reconciliation in the Treasury processes are properly segregated and as such, there remains an unmitigated risk that fraud, errors, or misstatement may occur. The Company should ensure the following (example nonkey language below) for remediation: Duties are appropriately segregated: • Authorization and execution of Treasury transactions are segregated from recording of transactions • Payment processing is segregated from review • Treasury reconciliation performance is segregated from reconciliation review To be implemented by Abivax in 2026

Gap- The Company does not perform or document an annual review of SOC 1 Type II reports for third-party and sub-service organizations. As a result, the adequacy and operating effectiveness of the third-party control environments are not evaluated. As such, there is an unmitigated risk that deficiencies or control failures at service organizations may go undetected, potentially resulting in errors or misstatements in the Company’s financial reporting. Below is the proposed control for remediation: SOC 1 reports are reviewed on an annual basis by the Company to ensure there are adequate controls at the third-party organizations and sub-service organizations and no significant deficiencies have been identified by the third party organization and sub-service organizations' external auditors. If there are deficiencies noted on the report, their significance is considered and evaluated. This review is evidenced via comments made on the Type II Report Workbook by the TBD by Management. For SOC 1 reports that are not through year-end, a bridge letter is obtained from the third party indicating that there are no material changes in the control environment through year-end.

Quarterly, the GL accountant prepares the short term investments accounts, to verify that the general ledger balances agree to supporting documentation (i.e., bank statements, contracts...) and account balances are complete and accurate. Significant variances (as defined by Management) are investigated and resolved. The Accounting Director reviews and approves the reconciliation as evidenced via email approval and comments throughout the spreadsheet.

Refer to FSCP: Quarterly, the AP Team prepares a prepaid expense workbook, including supporting documentation (invoices and contracts). The workbook is submitted via email to the Accounting Director/Senior GL Accountant for review and approval. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, notes, and sign-off within the reconciliation workbooks.

records.controlSummary: Gap - While there is communication between Management and the Third Party Tax Provider regarding changes to tax laws/regulations and other topics as they arise, it is ad-hoc and informal in nature. The Company should establish a formal cadence with the Third Party Tax Provider (at least quarterly), to discuss the results of business operations that could impact the tax provision, and any new or changes to tax pronouncements that may have accounting and/or reporting implications. This would mitigate risk that the tax provision calculation and related footnote disclosure is incomplete and/or inaccurate, and not in compliance with the prescribed ASC guidance. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly (beginning in Q4), a meeting is held between the representatives from the Company and the Third Party Tax Service Provider to discuss the results of business operations that are used to calculate the tax provision, and to evaluate any new or changes to tax laws or regulations. Evidence of the discussion is documented and retained via [insert method].

Enhancement - As the company transitions to being a quarterly US filer, a quarterly tax provision will be required. As an SEC registrant, the Company is required to disclose changes to their annual tax provision quarterly in the form 10Q/K. Until a quarterly review is performed to validate the accuracy of the Company's tax provision, there is an unmitigated risk that financial statements are incomplete, inaccurate, and/or not in compliance with prescribed guidance. Below is the proposed control for enhancement (note that the company currently prepares an annual provision) Quarterly, the Third Party Tax Provider prepares a memo documenting the Company's tax position to determine if a quarterly provision is required. The memo is reviewed and approved by the Finance Manager and SVP Finance as evidenced via notes and sign-off on the memo and/or email correspondence.

Annually, the Third-Party Tax Provider prepares the Tax Provision and Tax Provision Memo that is reviewed and approved by the Finance Manager and the SVP Finance. Evidence of review is via tickmarks, comments, and sign-off throughout the workbook and/or email correspondence.

Annually, the Third-Party Tax Provider prepares the deferred tax rollforward on a jurisdictional basis in conjunction with the year-end tax provision. The deferred tax rollforward is reviewed and approved by the Finance Manager and SVP Finance as part of the annual tax provision revision review [TAX-03]. Evidence of review is via signoff and comments/tickmarks throughout the file.

Annually, the Third-Party Tax Provider prepares the Return to Provision (RTP) analysis that is reviewed and approved by the Finance Manager and SVP Finance during review of the annual tax provision (TX-03). Evidence of review and approval is via email correspondence.

Annually, the Third Party Tax Provider prepares a memo to document the Company's tax position in the following areas: - Annual Tax Provision - Uncertain Tax Positions - Valuation Allowance The memo is reviewed and approved by the Finance Manager and SVP Finance as evidenced via tickmarks/comments throughout the file and email correspondence.

Annually, the Third-Party Tax Provider drafts the tax related financial disclosures, which are reviewed by the Finance Manager and SVP Finance prior to the financial statement filing. Evidence of the Finance manager / VP Finance's review and approval is via email correspondence.
Annually, the Board of Directors approves the hiring budget prepared by the Chief People Officer (CPO). Evidence of review and approval is via meeting minutes and/or written consent.

Offer letters for all non-executive employees are reviewed and approved by the CPO (within benchmark parameters) prior to being extended to a candidate. Outside-parameter offer letters require secondary approval by the Board, as evidenced via email. Offer letters for all executive employees are reviewed and approved by the Board, and via signoff on the offer letter by the CEO. Evidence of approval is Board meeting minutes and/or signoff by the CEO. [confirm] The Compensation Committee and the Board will approve any offer related to CEO and C-Suite executives.

records.controlSummary: As needed, for any new hires, the CPO (US) / VP HR (France) provides the New Hire Form via email to the various Departments for completion. The IT team reviews/approves the Form to verify the new hire has been approved, appropriate physical and logical access has been granted (including specific systems access and profiles per the supervisor), and that all HR and Payroll actions have been completed. The approved New Hire Form is saved in Egnyte.

As needed, upon signing of the Offer Letter, new hire data is entered into ADP by the HR Consultant or CPO. A dual-review process is performed for accuracy and completeness purposes - if the CPO performs the data entry, the HR Consultant conducts a review of the entered information as evidenced by email. If the HR Consultant performs the data entry, the CPO conducts the review. <note for Abivax - this is non-key and there remains a gap from an SOD perspective>

Monthly, the HR Consultant prepares an excel report of all employee masterdata changes (new or changes to existing employees) which is reviewed and approved by the CPO via email. As part of the review, the CPO agrees all employee changes to underlying approved support. Evidence of review of the analysis by the CPO is performed via email, comments throughout the spreadsheet, and physical or electronic sign-off on the spreadsheet.

As needed, an email containing a departures checklist is generated by the CPO (US) / VP HR (France) for completion by the required Department Heads and reviewed and approved by IT via sign off to verify all appropriate termination actions have been completed timely. The approved termination checklist is saved in Egnyte.

records.controlTitle: Review and approval of the monthly pay run
records.controlSummary: Semi-monthly, prior to approving the payment of payroll, a pre-process payroll register is prepared by the HR Consultant and reviewed by the Finance Manager, who performs a variance analysis on the payroll run (gross salary and deductions) and agrees employee changes to supporting documentation. The SVP Finance performs final review and approval via email.

records.controlTitle: Authorisation and release of wire payment
records.controlSummary: Semi-monthly, the SVP Finance approves the payroll run within the ADP system, up to $375,000. For payroll runs above $375,000, a wire transfer is prepared by the Finance Manager and approved by the SVP Finance or, CPO as designee as evidenced by the release of the wire.

Semi-monthly, the SVP Finance reviews and approves the payroll expense and payroll accruals journal entries posted to Sage. As part of the review, the SVP finance agrees the payroll report amounts to the journal entry details to validate completeness and accuracy. All discrepancies identified are investigated and resolved prior to approving the journal entry. Evidence of approval is via posting of the entry and signoff in the Reconciliation Workbook.

Quarterly, the Finance Manager prepares the Bonus accrual calculation and related journal entry. Gap- There is no formal evidence retained of the review and approval of the bonus accrual calculation and related journal entry. Below is the proposed control for remediation: Quarterly, the Finance Manager prepares the Bonus accrual calculation and related journal entry. The accrual calculation is reviewed and approved by the SVP Finance, as evidenced via sign-off via email.

Monthly, the Finance Manager prepares and the SVP Finance reviews and approves the payroll account reconciliations, to verify that the general ledger balances agree to supporting documentation and payroll account balances are complete and accurate. As part of the reconciliation, the Finance compares the payroll payment to the ADP invoice. Significant variances (as defined by Management) are investigated, evidenced through comments on the reconciliation. Matt to confirm what checks are performed as part of this month end payroll review. Month end reconciliation procedures by the Finance Manager should typically include: 1. Verifies the payroll registers, and bank statements are generated for the appropriate periods. 2. Verifies that the final payroll amounts on the ADP payroll registers and invoice(s) agrees to the cash disbursements on the bank statements. 3. Verifies that the disbursements from the bank account agree to general ledger. 4. Verifies that all journal entries are booked for the appropriate amount. 5. Verifies the GL Coding on all journal entries is appropriate. 6. Verifies any variances have been investigated, resolved, and documented. 7. Evidences review via tickmarks, comments throughout the reconciliation workbook and email communication. Note that this control (BS reconciliations) will remain in each process in year one, but consolidated as one control and moved to financial statement close (FSCP) in year two

records.controlSummary: Annually, the Chief People Officer prepares an excel based "annual review compensation" file containing merit increases and bonus payout calculations, which is reviewed by the Remuneration Committee, Legal Counsel and the Board of Directors, evidenced via meeting minutes and/or email.
